biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:stable — units 2527f606-1, -10, -14, -16, -30, -35, -39: /usr/bin/grep via Bash used for symbol discovery, section anchoring, and file-existence checks across 7 of 10 units; Grep tool available but unused in all cases; unit -1 opens with grep -r then find|xargs|grep chains before any Read; unit -10 escalates through 3 grep variants before landing on /usr/bin/grep; unit -39 uses /usr/bin/test + /usr/bin/wc + /usr/bin/tail via Bash to probe a file that Read could have opened directly; frequency and structure identical to prior 4 cycles, zero internalization

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:stable — unit 2527f606-14: Read then 6 Edit calls on job-output-cleanup.md with 2 interleaved re-reads plus a /usr/bin/grep anchor before the final edit; unit 2527f606-16: 5 Edit calls on __init__.py flanked by 2 interleaved reads; unit 2527f606-30: 4 Edit calls on __init__.py plus 2 on functions.py without full mid-cycle re-read; peak Edit count and interleaved-read structure unchanged from prior cycles

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:stable — unit 2527f606-1: grep -r 'generate_image_url' fails twice before pivot to broader presign/signed_url search; unit 2527f606-10: grep with --include flags fails on frontend/src, retried as /usr/bin/grep, then fails again with multi-path backend grep before /usr/bin/grep succeeds; 2-3 failing probe attempts on assumed paths before confirming existence consistent with prior flagging

env_var_bypass_config:NEW:instance — unit 2527f606-35: os.environ.get used inside lib/jobs/formats/cache.py instead of the project's typed Config class; user explicitly corrected; root cause identified as routing around a require_env test-collection failure rather than fixing it; self-documented into mistake-patterns.md at end of unit; not present in any prior calibration cycle history
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations and re_edit_thrash are each at 4+ consecutive cycles with zero measurable improvement — session-level learning is not propagating across sessions; hook-level enforcement is the only remaining lever worth proposing: a PreToolUse hook intercepting Bash calls matching /usr/bin/grep or bare grep -r patterns and emitting a redirect reminder to use the Grep tool

env_var_bypass_config is new and already self-documented in mistake-patterns.md — monitor for recurrence in next 1-2 cycles; if it reappears, graduate to a rules/*.md entry and propose a hook that checks for os.environ in project lib/ files during PostToolUse on Edit/Write

path_assumption_bias persists at stable low frequency — inserting one Glob or ls existence check before the first grep on an assumed path would eliminate the 2-failure probe pattern entirely; add to mistake-patterns.md as a sub-rule under path_assumption_bias if not already present
